title: Centaurium exaltatum
intro: Centaurium exaltatum, commonly known as tall centaury, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the Gentianaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium exaltatum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Centaurium exaltatum is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is commonly used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Centaurium exaltatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is commonly known as tall centaury. The genus Centaurium is a part of the tribe Gentianeae. The exact classification might be further detailed in more specific taxonomic systems. However, it is generally recognized as a member of the Gentianaceae family.
uses: Centaurium exaltatum is traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties have been studied for potential benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. It is also believed to have antiviral properties, which may help in the treatment of certain viral infections. Additionally, Centaurium exaltatum is reported to have antioxidant and antifungal properties. Its medicinal uses are still being researched, and more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness.

constituents: Centaurium exaltatum, also known as tall centaury, is a flowering plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The active constituents of Centaurium exaltatum include iridoids, such as centaureidin, centaurin C1, and centaurin C2.
